risks: Cache storage account uses Standard_LRS — Microsoft recommends ZRS for zone-resilient ASR replication to avoid data loss during availability zone failures
No monitoring or diagnostics resources (Log Analytics workspace, diagnostic settings, alerts) for operational visibility into VMs, ASR replication health, or Automation runbook execution
Automation runbook DNS failover is trigger-dependent; a runbook execution failure could leave DNS records pointing to an unavailable region
No RBAC role assignments defined for the Automation Account system-assigned managed identity to operate on the DNS zone or Recovery Services vault
Missing ASR recovery plan resource (Microsoft.RecoveryServices/vaults/replicationRecoveryPlans) to orchestrate multi-VM failover with runbook pre/post actions
No replicationProtectedItems resources to actually enable VM replication within the ASR fabric
Automation Account has publicNetworkAccess enabled and disableLocalAuth set to false, reducing security posture
correctionsRecommended: Upgrade cache storage account SKU from Standard_LRS to Standard_ZRS for availability zone resilience as recommended by Microsoft for ASR cache storage
Add a Log Analytics workspace and configure diagnostic settings on VMs, Recovery Services vault, and Automation Account for centralized monitoring
Add explicit RBAC role assignments (DNS Zone Contributor on the DNS zone, Site Recovery Contributor on the RSV) for the Automation Account system-assigned managed identity
Add an ASR recovery plan resource (Microsoft.RecoveryServices/vaults/replicationRecoveryPlans) to orchestrate failover sequence with the DNS runbook as a pre/post action
Consider replacing the DNS zone + Automation runbook pattern with Azure Traffic Manager or Azure Front Door for automatic health-probe-based DNS failover
Set disableLocalAuth to true and publicNetworkAccess to false on the Automation Account for security hardening
Add resource tags across all resources for cost tracking, environment identification, and operational management
